mission: Govern Hugging Face adoption for RAMIN OS without exposing sensitive data.

official_references.name: Inference Providers
official_references.why_it_matters: Hosted Inference Providers and OpenAI-compatible APIs are useful for public-data PoCs, not a free production backbone.

official_references.name: Hub API for OpenAI-compatible models
official_references.why_it_matters: The router can list chat models and provider metadata for governed comparison.

official_references.name: Hugging Face MCP Server
official_references.why_it_matters: Hub model, dataset, Space, documentation, and paper search can become a read-only discovery tool.

official_references.name: Spaces as MCP servers
official_references.why_it_matters: Public Spaces can expose tools, but they must be treated as untrusted external execution.

official_references.name: hf CLI and skills
official_references.why_it_matters: The hf CLI is the standard local control surface for Hub repos, downloads, and agent skills.

official_references.name: Text Embeddings Inference
official_references.why_it_matters: TEI is the strongest private RAG/embedding path for internal documents and customer-adjacent retrieval.

local_readiness.note: Credential presence is intentionally not inspected by HF Radar.

operating_principle: Hosted HF services are for public/synthetic PoCs; internal documents and customer data use local or self-hosted models only.

system_fit_summary.best_variant: Private RAG first, HF discovery second, hosted inference last.
system_fit_summary.why_not_api_first: Free hosted inference is limited and external. The professional move is to govern model selection and build private retrieval/serving before routing sensitive work anywhere.

recommendation.name: Private RAG Embedding Layer (TEI + HF embedding models)

opportunity.name: Private RAG Embedding Layer (TEI + HF embedding models)
opportunity.use_case: internal document search, Brain retrieval, policy/FAQ retrieval, CX evidence lookup
opportunity.description: Self-host Text Embeddings Inference with open embedding models so RAMIN OS can search its own documents without sending sensitive content to public endpoints.
risks: model quality must be benchmarked on Azerbaijani, Turkish, Russian, and insurance-domain text
local CPU/GPU capacity can limit latency
model licenses and versions must be pinned
controls: Use open-license embedding models only.
Keep the embedding server on localhost or a private host.
Do not upload customer data to public Spaces or hosted providers.
Run retrieval quality tests before replacing current Brain search.

reasons: Private/self-host path can handle sensitive RAMIN OS data.
High strategic fit for RAMIN OS.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Use open-license embedding models only.
Keep the embedding server on localhost or a private host.
Do not upload customer data to public Spaces or hosted providers.
Run retrieval quality tests before replacing current Brain search.

opportunity.name: Private CX Sentiment Classifier
opportunity.use_case: customer complaint sentiment reinforcement for CX triage
opportunity.description: Use a local/private Hugging Face text-classification endpoint as an additional CX sentiment signal. It can elevate negative risk, but deterministic rules remain the safety baseline.
risks: off-the-shelf multilingual sentiment models can misread Azerbaijani complaint nuance
model drift or weak local endpoint availability must not weaken CX risk handling
controls: Keep deterministic CX rules as the baseline.
Allow HF sentiment to raise risk, not lower rule-based complaint risk.
Use local/private endpoints for customer messages.
Benchmark labels against Azerbaijani complaint examples before operational use.

reasons: Private/self-host path can handle sensitive RAMIN OS data.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Keep deterministic CX rules as the baseline.
Allow HF sentiment to raise risk, not lower rule-based complaint risk.
Use local/private endpoints for customer messages.
Benchmark labels against Azerbaijani complaint examples before operational use.

opportunity.name: Local Open-Weight LLM Serving (llama.cpp/vLLM/SGLang)
opportunity.use_case: private drafting, local analysis, fallback model for gateway and AI Council
opportunity.description: Run selected open-weight HF models behind an OpenAI-compatible local endpoint for tasks that must stay inside RAMIN OS.
risks: model hallucination and weak Azerbaijani reasoning must be measured
Windows/corporate hardware may constrain useful model size
large model downloads need explicit operator approval
controls: Keep private tier routed to local endpoints only.
Benchmark against current Gemini/Groq outputs before promotion.
Record model id, quantization, license, and hardware requirements.

reasons: Private/self-host path can handle sensitive RAMIN OS data.
High strategic fit for RAMIN OS.
Needs an explicit implementation checkpoint before rollout.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Keep private tier routed to local endpoints only.
Benchmark against current Gemini/Groq outputs before promotion.
Record model id, quantization, license, and hardware requirements.

opportunity.name: HF MCP + hf CLI Discovery Workbench
opportunity.use_case: model, dataset, Space, paper, and documentation discovery for agents
opportunity.description: Give Codex/Claude-style agents a read-only Hugging Face research surface through the HF MCP Server and hf CLI skills, while keeping execution and credentials out of automatic flows.
risks: MCP tools can expand agent capability faster than governance if installed casually
write-capable tokens could upload or mutate Hub repos
controls: Use read-only/fine-grained Hugging Face tokens for discovery.
Run candidate models, Spaces, and agents through Agent Radar before use.
Do not connect MCP tools to customer-data workflows.

reasons: External service/tooling is acceptable only for public or synthetic data.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Use read-only/fine-grained Hugging Face tokens for discovery.
Run candidate models, Spaces, and agents through Agent Radar before use.
Do not connect MCP tools to customer-data workflows.
Use fine-grained/read-only tokens unless a human approves a write-capable workflow.

opportunity.name: Public Spaces API/MCP for Media Experiments
opportunity.use_case: music, SFX, TTS, image/video PoCs, and creative research
opportunity.description: Continue using public HF Spaces as cheap media R&D surfaces, but classify them as external sandbox tools with public or synthetic inputs only.
risks: public Spaces can change API signatures, sleep, or disappear
media prompts can accidentally include brand-confidential details
MCP-enabled Spaces are untrusted external tools
controls: Treat Spaces as sandbox providers, not core production infrastructure.
Keep provider ids configurable in environment/example files.
Never send customer records, claims, or private creative strategy to public Spaces.

reasons: External service/tooling is acceptable only for public or synthetic data.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Treat Spaces as sandbox providers, not core production infrastructure.
Keep provider ids configurable in environment/example files.
Never send customer records, claims, or private creative strategy to public Spaces.

opportunity.name: smolagents / tiny-agents Sandbox
opportunity.use_case: small governed agents that can call internal tools during auditions
opportunity.description: Use HF agent frameworks only inside RAMIN OS sandbox auditions. They are useful for tool experiments, but the execution boundary matters more than the framework.
risks: code-executing agents can perform unintended file/network actions
MCP or Space tools can broaden permissions quickly
framework demos often skip enterprise audit controls
controls: Agent Radar intake is mandatory before any sandbox trial.
Read-only tools first; no write/send/payment actions.
Run with logs, no secrets, no production credentials, and explicit human review.

reasons: External service/tooling is acceptable only for public or synthetic data.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Agent Radar intake is mandatory before any sandbox trial.
Read-only tools first; no write/send/payment actions.
Run with logs, no secrets, no production credentials, and explicit human review.

opportunity.name: HF Router PoC for Public Prompts
opportunity.use_case: quick non-sensitive model comparison for public marketing prompts
opportunity.description: Use Hugging Face Inference Providers/Router only for public-data experiments and model comparison. It is not treated as unlimited free production capacity.
risks: free credit is small and not reliable for production
external providers can have their own retention/security policies
routing can select a provider that is unsuitable for regulated data
controls: Block customer data, policy documents, and claims data from this route.
Log provider/model usage through the existing LLM usage board.
Keep a local/private fallback for sensitive tasks.

reasons: External service/tooling is acceptable only for public or synthetic data.
Useful for comparison, but not reliable as unlimited free production API.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Block customer data, policy documents, and claims data from this route.
Log provider/model usage through the existing LLM usage board.
Keep a local/private fallback for sensitive tasks.
Use fine-grained/read-only tokens unless a human approves a write-capable workflow.

opportunity.name: Brand/Domain LoRA Track
opportunity.use_case: future Xalq visual style models, Azerbaijani insurance-domain adapters, controlled fine-tuning
opportunity.description: Prepare a governed dataset path before any HF-hosted training or LoRA workflow. This can be a quality leap later, but it needs dataset rights, redaction, and approval first.
risks: training data can leak private brand, customer, or licensed material
dataset rights and model license terms must be reviewed
hosted training may persist artifacts outside RAMIN OS
controls: Create a curated dataset manifest before any training.
Use only licensed or internally approved assets.
Human approval required before upload, training, or publication.

reasons: External path conflicts with sensitive-data handling.
Needs an explicit implementation checkpoint before rollout.
Training/fine-tuning requires dataset governance before any upload.
required_controls: No production use without a RAMIN OS sandbox/audit pass.
No secrets or credentials in reports, prompts, logs, or public Spaces.
Create a curated dataset manifest before any training.
Use only licensed or internally approved assets.
Human approval required before upload, training, or publication.
Use fine-grained/read-only tokens unless a human approves a write-capable workflow.
Sensitive or customer data must stay out of this workflow.

next_actions: Pilot TEI/local embeddings for Brain and internal document retrieval.
Install/use HF MCP and hf CLI only as read-only discovery surfaces.
Keep HF Router tests limited to public marketing prompts and synthetic examples.
Run every public Space, MCP server, or agent framework through Agent Radar before use.
Create a dataset governance checklist before any LoRA, upload, or fine-tuning workflow.
